page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c16g1.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs based on the Intel Xeon 8163 processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 62.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.88 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA T4 GPU based on the Turing architecture, offering 16 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25 percent, alongside poor single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, with Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores landing in the middle 50 percent. The primary strength of this instance lies in its GPU-driven capabilities. In LLM inference benchmarks, prompt processing for both 135M and 7B models achieves top-tier performance in the top 10 percent, while text generation for a 7B model is rated as strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.gn6i-c16g1.4xlarge offers a balanced entry point for GPU-accelerated workloads without the high cost of top-tier enterprise accelerators. The tradeoff involves lower single-core CPU performance and the lack of local storage, which requires relying on network-attached storage. This instance is best suited for workloads such as small to medium LLM inference, machine learning model deployment, video transcoding, and moderate multi-threaded computing tasks that can leverage the NVIDIA T4 GPU.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c16g1.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, 62.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of VRAM. Built on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, the instance does not include local storage and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core CPU performance, but multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The instance excels in GPU-driven tasks, demonstrating top-tier prompt processing speeds for small and medium LLMs. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for machine learning inference, video processing, and parallel computing workloads that benefit from GPU acceleration while maintaining a balanced resource-to-cost ratio.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6i-c16g1.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 62 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU, optimized for machine learning inference and parallel computing.

tagline: An x86 GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA T4 GPU and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for efficient machine learning inference.

1x NVIDIA T4 GPU with 16 GB of dedicated VRAM
62.0 GB of system memory and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier prompt processing performance for small and medium LLMs
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage
Best suited for machine learning inference, video transcoding, and parallel computing
